methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Manifold Bio is a Series A-stage biotech company founded by Gleb Kuznetsov and Pierce Ogden, having raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 entities: 3 public-market biotech peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private companies in the same sector at comparable or adjacent stages, including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, using the $40M total raised as a proxy baseline, implying a $80M valuation. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $805M median valuation, with a range of $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Manifold Bio's revenue is not on file, a common limitation for private companies. The public sector median P/S of 5.8x across the three tickers therefore remains an unused cross-check.

The two applicable methods produce a wide valuation range of $68M to $926M, with a median of $442M. This dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty regarding Manifold's market positioning relative to the peer set. The stage-progression method yields the floor; peer comparables drive the ceiling. Key uncertainties include stage-relative positioning within the peer cohort and revenue trajectory needed to justify higher multiples.
